items.q: How do stakeholder and shareholder interests overlap and conflict?
items.correct: Shareholders are a subset of stakeholders. Their interests overlap (long-term success benefits all) but conflict on issues like dividends vs investment, wages vs profits, or short-term gains vs long-term sustainability.
items.misconception: Stakeholders and shareholders always want the same thing.
items.random_wrong: Shareholders are external and stakeholders are internal.
items.why: The shareholder-stakeholder distinction is central to debates on corporate purpose. Students miss the structural tension — a board can't always please everyone simultaneously, which is what stakeholder analysis exists to examine.

items.correct: Shareholders' personal assets are protected if the company fails — they can only lose the amount they invested, not their personal wealth.
items.misconception: Limited liability means the company has limited debts it can take on.
items.random_wrong: Limited liability means a business can only operate for a limited time.
items.why: Limited liability is about the LIMIT on what shareholders can lose, not on what the company can do. Students misread the term as restricting the business rather than protecting investors.

items.q: What three Ps are added to make the extended marketing mix (7Ps)?
items.correct: People (staff/service quality), Process (how the service is delivered), and Physical Evidence (tangible cues — uniforms, packaging, branding) — added to make the 4Ps relevant to services marketing.
items.misconception: The 7Ps are just the 4Ps repeated with different words.
items.random_wrong: The 7Ps include Profit, Planning and Performance.
items.why: The extended mix specifically addresses services, where customer experience depends on staff, process and atmospherics — not just product. Students treat 4Ps and 7Ps as interchangeable, missing what services marketing requires.

items.q: Porter's three generic strategies are cost leadership, differentiation and:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Focus. “Diversification” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: The 'Blue Ocean' concept was proposed by:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Kim and Mauborgne. “Porter” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many generic strategies did Porter identify?
items.why: The real figure is 3 strategies. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
